title: Understanding Angular Momentum Conservation
text: The gymnast's angular momentum is conserved when she transitions from cartwheels to flips in the air. This means that her initial angular momentum is equal to her angular momentum while flipping.

title: Calculate Initial Angular Momentum
text: Use the formula for angular momentum: \( L $$=$$ I \cdot \omega \), where \( I \) is the moment of inertia and \( \omega \) is the angular velocity. Calculate the initial angular momentum when she is doing cartwheels.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity in the Air
text: Use the conservation of angular momentum: \( L_{initial} $$=$$ L_{final} \). Solve for the angular velocity \( \omega_{final} \) when she is in the tuck position.

title: Final Angular Velocity Calculation
text: Given: \( L_{initial} $$=$$ $$6.75$$ \) and \( I_{final} $$=$$ $$3.4$$ \) $$kg m^2$$. Calculate \( \omega_{final} \).

title: Calculate Number of Revolutions
text: Use the formula for the number of revolutions: \( \text{Revolutions} $$=$$ \omega_{final} \times \text{time} \).

title: Revolutions Calculation
text: Given: \( \omega_{final} $$=$$ $$1.985$$ \) $$\frac{rev}{s}$$ and \( \text{time} $$=$$ $$2.0$$ \) s. Calculate the number of revolutions.
